claim: Short sleep increases hunger and late-night eating.
plainLanguageSummary: Sleeping less than 7 hours can make you hungrier and more likely to eat late at night.
appImplication: Coach sleep before tightening calorie targets.

claim: Higher protein helps preserve lean mass during energy deficit.
plainLanguageSummary: Eating more protein while cutting calories helps keep muscle, especially with exercise.
appImplication: Personalize protein targets by age, activity, and deficit size.

claim: Ultra-processed foods drive overeating independently of macros.
plainLanguageSummary: People eating processed foods ate ~500 more calories per day than those eating whole foods, even when meals were matched.
appImplication: Food quality and meal structure matter beyond calorie counting.

claim: Waist circumference adds cardiometabolic risk information beyond BMI.
plainLanguageSummary: Where you carry fat matters. Waist measurement adds risk info that weight/BMI alone misses.
appImplication: Track waist alongside weight. Prioritize trend over daily scale noise.

claim: Balancing sodium and potassium reduces blood pressure and cardiovascular risk.
plainLanguageSummary: Less salt, more potassium-rich foods like beans, spinach, potatoes, and bananas helps blood pressure.
appImplication: Flag high-sodium patterns and suggest potassium-rich alternatives.

claim: The body adapts to calorie deficits over time, slowing weight loss.
plainLanguageSummary: As you lose weight, your body fights back by burning fewer calories. Static targets stop working.
appImplication: Adjust targets from trend data rather than keeping static prescriptions.

claim: More daily steps are linked to lower all-cause and cardiovascular mortality.
plainLanguageSummary: Walking more reduces risk. Benefits start well below 10,000 steps.
appImplication: Personalize step goals progressively, not one-size-fits-all.

claim: Menstrual cycle effects on performance are highly individual.
plainLanguageSummary: There is no one-size-fits-all training prescription based on cycle phase.
appImplication: Be cycle-aware, not cycle-deterministic. Track symptoms, allow flexibility.

claim: Resistance training plus adequate protein supports strength and function in older adults.
plainLanguageSummary: After 60, muscle tends to decrease. Strength training and enough protein help maintain function.
appImplication: Include function-first older adult mode with protein and resistance emphasis.

claim: Several medication classes can affect appetite, fluid balance, or weight trend.
plainLanguageSummary: Insulin, some antidepressants, antipsychotics, and steroids can change weight. This is a body response, not a failure.
appImplication: Ask about medications during onboarding. Adapt coaching language.

claim: South Africa has the highest obesity rate in the African Region, with strong dietary transitions.
plainLanguageSummary: About 3 in 10 SA adults are obese. Sugar-sweetened beverage intake is high, and fruit/vegetable intake is low.
appImplication: SA local food support, portion guides, and culturally relevant coaching are essential.
